text: Thrilled to announce that I have defended my PhD titled *LLMs as Sociotechnical Actors: A Scaffolding Framework for Recovering Context Lost in Text-Only Settings*. Grateful to everyone who supported this work!
The [PATHs](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.16023) paper received a **Senior Area Chair Highlight** award at EMNLP 2025!

Started as a Senior Applied Scientist at [Microsoft](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/group/office-of-applied-research/) and moved to the Seattle area! I will continue working on human-agent collaboration for productivity.
Our paper [PATHs](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.16023) is accepted to EMNLP 2025! See you in Suzhou, China.
[LawFlow](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.18942) is accepted to COLM 2025! See you in Montreal.
[Thinking Like A Lawyer In The Age Of Generative AI: Cognitive Limits On AI Adoption Among Lawyers](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5260645) is out on SSRN.
[LawFlow](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.18942), which captures lawyers' end-to-end thought processes, is out on ArXiv!

Gave a talk titled *When AI Learns to Listen: Generative AI in the Social Sciences* to high school students at the [Sparkway Learning PythonAI Workshop 2024](https://sites.google.com/view/sparkwaylearning/home).
Honored to be a speaker at the [Women in AI and Data Science Conference 2024](https://sites.google.com/umn.edu/dsi-wiads/speakers), discussing the impact of artifacts in LLM-generated data.
Thrilled to be interning at the [Office of Applied Research at Microsoft](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/group/office-of-applied-research/) this fall!
Received the *2024 AnitaB.org Advancing Inclusion Scholarship*!

Our paper *Publics' Perceptions of Legitimacy in Corporate Social Advocacy: A Computational Analysis of the Influence of Ideological Congruence* is published in [Public Relations Review](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0363811124000651).
Gave a talk titled *Beyond Words: Incorporating Graph Data into LLMs for Comprehensive Social System Analysis* at [IC2S2 2024](https://ic2s2-2024.org/schedule).
Presented [Which Modality should I use?](https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-naacl.34/) at NAACL 2024 in Mexico City! [[Tweet]](https://x.com/geekylildeb/status/1803542147460690103)
Thrilled to be interning at [Microsoft Research](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/group/office-of-applied-research/) this summer!
Presented my [recent paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.14698) on LLM-generated artifacts at Megagon Labs, along with [Dongyeop Kang](https://dykang.github.io/).
Passed my Thesis Proposal Exam :) One more exam to go until my defense!
Abstract *Beyond Words: Incorporating Graph Data into LLMs for Comprehensive Social System Analysis* selected for oral presentation at IC2S2 2024!
[Which Modality should I use — Text, Motif, or Image? Understanding Graphs with Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09862) is accepted at NAACL 2024 Findings!

[Under the Surface: Tracking the Artifactuality of LLM-Generated Data](https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.14698) is out on ArXiv. This paper marks my first venture as a project team lead and MinnesotaNLP's first lab-wide project!

New preprint at the intersection of graphs and LLMs — [Which Modality should I use — Text, Motif, or Image? Understanding Graphs with Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09862) is out on ArXiv!
Our paper [Balancing the Effect of Training Dataset Distribution of Multiple Styles for Multi-Style Text Transfer](https://aclanthology.org/2023.findings-acl.243/) was presented at ACL 2023 Findings.
Our work on *Rebuilding Social Connection and Enhancing Advertising Effects Through the Nostalgic Appeal during the Pandemic* was accepted to AEJMC 2023.

Our work on *Work In Progress: Reactions to Incivility: A Computational Approach to Measuring Affective Polarization on Twitter During the First 2020 U.S. Presidential Debate* was accepted to ICA 2023.
Our work on *Publics' Perceptions of Legitimacy in Corporate Social Advocacy: A Computational Analysis of the Influence of Ideological Congruence* was accepted to ICA 2023.
Our paper [AdBERT: An Effective Few Shot Learning Framework for Aligning Tweets to Superbowl Advertisements](https://aclanthology.org/2022.wnut-1.19.pdf) was presented at WNUT @ COLING 2022.
